task_completion.description: Log in to the Splunk Enterprise web interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Splunk Enterprise web interface loaded in a browser, indicating a successful login (e.g., the Splunk home screen or navigation bar is visible).
A dashboard with the exact title 'Security_Investigation_Workbook' is visible in the Splunk UI.

task_completion.description: Include a time range picker input on the dashboard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A time range dropdown/picker is visible in the inputs section at the top of the dashboard.

task_completion.description: Include a text input for the target IP address with the token 'target_ip' and default value '*'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text input field is visible on the dashboard. In edit mode or the source XML view, the input is shown to be configured with the token 'target_ip' and a default value of '*'.

task_completion.description: Create at least three visualization panels on the dashboard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dashboard displays a minimum of three distinct visualization panels (e.g., charts, tables, or single values).

task_completion.description: Configure panel queries to use the required indexes and token substitution.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the dashboard edit mode, search bar, or source XML view, the queries for the panels explicitly show 'index=security_logs' or 'index=web_logs' and include the token substitution (e.g., '$target_ip$').

integrity.description: The agent used the Splunk web interface to create the dashboard.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Splunk Web UI in a browser, rather than purely using terminal commands to generate XML files.

integrity.description: The dashboard is genuinely rendered within Splunk.
integrity.visual_evidence: The dashboard appears within the standard Splunk application framework with native Splunk UI elements, not as a standalone HTML file or text document.

integrity.description: The agent actually configured the queries rather than hardcoding visual results.
integrity.visual_evidence: The panels show actual Splunk visualizations (or 'waiting for data' / 'no results found' states typical of Splunk) rather than pasted static text or images.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Dashboard Name must be exactly 'Security_Investigation_Workbook'. The dashboard must contain at least 2 inputs (a time range picker and a text input) and at least 3 visualization panels. The text input must use the token 'target_ip' with a default value of '*'. The panel queries must target either 'security_logs' or 'web_logs' indexes and include the token substitution (e.g., $target_ip$).
